ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent on which the country is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  "gdp_nominal" REAL /* Nominal GDP of the country */,
  "gdp_purchasing_power_parity" REAL /* GDP of the country in terms of purchasing power parity */,
  "inflation_rate" REAL /* Inflation rate of the country */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "labor_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each labor statistic entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "hours_worked" REAL /* Average hours worked in a year per worker */,
  "real_wage" REAL /* Real wage adjusted for inflation */,
  "gdp_per_hour" REAL /* GDP per hour worked */,
  "productivity_growth" REAL /* Growth rate of productivity */,
  "labor_force_participation_rate" REAL /* Labor force participation rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_labor_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "education_levels" (
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each education level */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Level of education (e.g., High School, Bachelor's) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the education level */,
  "education_field" TEXT /* Field of education (e.g., STEM, Humanities) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("education_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_education" (
  "map_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the mapping */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "education_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the education level */,
  "percentage" REAL /* Percentage of the population with the given education level */,
  "gender_ratio" REAL /* Ratio of males to females with the given education level */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("map_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_education_education_id FOREIGN KEY ("education_id") REFERENCES education_levels ("education_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_country_education_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the country */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the economic indicator */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the indicator is reported */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the economic indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_economic_indicators_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, economist, admin) */,
  "organization" TEXT /* Organization the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "query_string" TEXT /* Query string used to access the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_access_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the report */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "publish_date" TEXT /* Date the report was published */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., analysis, forecast, policy brief) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the report */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who commented */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date the comment was posted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_comments_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_imports" (
  "import_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data import */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "import_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was imported */,
  "data_format" TEXT /* Format of the imported data (e.g., CSV, Excel) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("import_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_imports_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policy_simulations" (
  "simulation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policy simulation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the simulation */,
  "simulation_name" TEXT /* Name of the simulation */,
  "scenario_description" TEXT /* Description of the scenario simulated */,
  "results" TEXT /* Results of the simulation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("simulation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policy_simulations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
